title: Electrical connector
abstract: An electronic device can include a housing forming an aperture within an exterior surface of the housing. An electronic component can be disposed within the housing and include a receptacle coupled to the electronic component. The housing and the receptacle can form an electrical port. The electronic device can further include a first engagement feature and a cable having a first plug connector and a second plug connector. The first plug connector can include a boot, a plug, and a second engagement feature. The boot is receivable within the aperture formed within the exterior surface of the housing. The plug is receivable within the receptacle to electrically couple the electronic device with the cable. The second engagement feature can interconnect or otherwise engage the first engagement feature to retain the cable to the electronic device.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An electrical port for receiving a plug connector including a boot, comprising: a trim ring including an internal wall at least partially defining a cavity, the cavity configured to substantially surround the boot; a receptacle coupled to the trim ring further defining the cavity; and an engagement feature disposed within the cavity configured to interlock with the plug connector, the engagement feature comprising an elongate member disposed within the receptacle, the elongate member defining a distal end configured to interlock with the plug connector. 2. The electrical port of claim 1, wherein: the trim ring further comprises an external surface; the boot includes a top surface; and the external surface is substantially parallel with the top surface when the distal end of the elongate member is interlocked with the plug connector. 3. The electrical port of claim 1, wherein: the trim ring defines a first aperture; the receptacle defines a second aperture; and the electrical port further comprises a fastener at least partially disposed within the first aperture and the second aperture. 4. The electrical port of claim 1, wherein the elongate member comprises a protrusion formed on the internal wall of the trim ring. - 37 - 5. The electrical port of claim 1, wherein the elongate member extends through the internal wall to interlock with the plug connector. 6. The electrical port of claim 1, wherein the engagement feature further comprises a magnet disposed within the cavity. 7. The electrical port of claim 1, wherein the engagement feature further comprises a canted coil spring. 8. The electrical port of claim 1, wherein the internal wall is sized to frictionally engage the boot. 9. The electrical port of claim 7, wherein the canted coil spring exerts a force on the elongate member. 10. The electrical port of claim 1, further comprising a seal positioned in the cavity. 11. A plug connector for a cable, comprising: a boot; a plug extending from the boot; and an engagement recess defined by an exterior surface of the boot, the engagement recess configured to interlock with an electrical port of an electronic device. 12. The plug connector of claim 11, wherein the engagement recess further comprises a canted coil spring at least partially disposed within a channel defined on an exterior surface of the boot. 13. The plug connector of claim 11, wherein the engagement recess comprises a first recess defined on a first side of the boot and a second recess defined on a second opposite side of the boot. 14. The plug connector of claim 11, wherein the engagement recess comprises a channel extending around a periphery of the boot. 15. The plug connector of claim 11, wherein the engagement feature further comprises a magnet disposed within the boot. 16. The plug connector of claim 11, wherein the engagement feature comprises a first engagement feature, and further comprising a second engagement feature defined by the Plug- 17. A cable assembly for an electronic device, comprising: a cable; a plug connector attached to the cable, the plug connector including a boot; and a plug extending from the boot and electrically connected to the cable; and an engagement feature defined in the plug, the engagement feature comprising: a recess defined by the plug; and a canted coil spring disposed in the recess. 18. The cable assembly of claim 17, wherein: the plug comprises a first surface, and a second surface substantially parallel to the first surface; the plug comprises an electrical contact; the canted coil spring encircles a periphery of the plug. 19. The cable assembly of claim 18, wherein: the engagement feature is a first engagement feature on a first side of the plug, and the plug connector further comprises a second engagement feature on a second side of the plug opposite the first side; and the electrical contact is a first electrical contact and the plug further comprises a second electrical contact. 20. The cable assembly of claim 18, wherein the recess defined by the plug extends around a periphery of the plug.
